sources.name: DKC Atlas Forum — Physics Constants
sources.notes: ROM-confirmed 8.8 fixed-point values for jump velocity (0xF9.00), walk (0x02.00), run (0x03.00), gravity holding B (0x00.48), gravity not holding B (0x00.70).

sources.name: TASVideos Game Resources — SNES / Donkey Kong Country
sources.notes: Supplemental mechanics documentation and TAS notes.

notes: DKC uses an 8.8 fixed-point physics system (256 sub-pixels per pixel), same precision as NES Mega Man.
All fixed-point hex values use signed 8.8 notation: 0xF9.00 means the integer byte is 0xF9 = 249 unsigned = -7 in two's complement signed 8-bit.
Jump initial velocity 0xF9.00 = -7.0 px/frame (upward direction is negative Y in this system).
GRAVITY IS VARIABLE based on jump button hold state — this dual-gravity design is the key to DKC's jump feel.
Two-character system: Donkey Kong (slower, stronger) and Diddy Kong (faster, weaker). Both use the same base physics.
DK roll and Diddy cartwheel are separate movement modes with distinct physics.
Animal buddy mounts change movement capabilities dramatically during their sections.

subpixel_system.description: Same 256 sub-pixel system as NES Mega Man. Signed 8.8: integer part is signed two's complement (0xF9 = -7), fractional part is 0–255 (0.00 to 0.99609375).

display.notes: DKC uses 32×32 pixel tiles for terrain — 2× the standard 16×16. This creates the characteristic large, chunky level geometry. Characters appear large relative to most SNES platformers.

walk_speed.notes: Base walk speed without holding Y. Instant velocity — no acceleration ramp.

run_speed.notes: Speed when holding Y (run/attack button). Run is immediate — no ramp needed to reach run speed. Run is standard traversal for most players.

notes: DK roll and Diddy cartwheel temporarily exceed run speed.
Roll can be initiated on ground while running. Maintains higher speed through the roll animation.
Can roll off ledges — airborne roll maintains momentum.
Exact roll speed needs ROM verification. Community estimate: ~4-5 px/f.
VERIFY against DKC Atlas ROM data.

ground_movement.notes: Walk and run are instant velocity. No acceleration curves for standard movement.

notes: 0xF9 in two's complement signed 8-bit = -7. Jump initial velocity is -7 px/frame (upward).
This is the highest jump initial velocity in the documented set — DKC jumps are tall.
At 7 px/frame initial velocity with ~0.28 px/f² gravity (held): peak height ≈ 7² / (2 × 0.28) ≈ 87.5 pixels ≈ 2.73 tiles (32px tiles).
With unhold gravity (0.4375): peak ≈ 7² / (2 × 0.4375) ≈ 56 pixels ≈ 1.75 tiles.

jump.notes: Jump height is variable. Holding jump keeps the lower gravity active (0.28125 px/f²). Releasing jump applies the higher gravity (0.4375 px/f²).
jump.horizontal_speed_during_jump: maintains ground speed at jump initiation (walk or run)

gravity._design_note: DKC's dual-gravity system is the defining physics feature. The ratio between the two gravity states creates the characteristic floaty-on-hold, snappy-on-release jump feel.

holding_jump_button.notes: 0x00.48 = 72/256 = 0.28125 px/frame². Active while the jump button is held. Creates the 'floaty' extended arc.

not_holding_jump_button.notes: 0x00.70 = 112/256 = 0.4375 px/frame². Active when jump button is released. Snaps player down faster for short jumps.

ratio.description: Not-holding gravity is ~1.56x holding gravity. Releasing jump at different points produces distinctly different arcs — this dynamic range makes DKC jumping feel expressive.

notes: Primary character. Slower but has longer reach.
Can throw barrels as projectiles (requires finding barrel).
When DK and Diddy are both present: DK leads, one hit loses Diddy. If Diddy already gone, one hit loses a life.
DK carries Diddy on back when barrel crate of Diddy is broken.

roll.description: DK roll: taps jump then left/right while running to initiate a roll on ground or air.
roll.notes: Roll maintains momentum and defeats most standard enemies. Can extend horizontal distance by rolling off ledges.

notes: Faster and slightly higher jumping than DK.
Cartwheel attack instead of barrel throw.
Starts as the character in control when the team first enters a stage.

cartwheel.description: Diddy cartwheel: equivalent to DK roll but slightly faster. Also usable to defeat enemies.

tag_team.description: Both Kongs present = 2-hit buffer before losing a life.
notes: Taking a hit loses the active Kong — the other runs off and must be rescued from a DK/Diddy barrel.
Two-hit system makes DKC more forgiving than single-hit games like Castlevania.
Pairs well with the floaty jump — the difficulty comes from precision, and the 2-hit buffer softens mistakes.

animal_buddies._note: Animal buddies radically change movement physics for their sections. Each creates a mini-paradigm within the level.

rambi.description: Rhino. Higher run speed, destroys most enemies and some cracked walls on contact.

expresso.description: Ostrich. Can flap wings to glide and slow descent. Cannot stomp normal enemies.
expresso.jump_notes: Higher jump than standard. Wing flap provides sustained aerial control.
expresso.notes: Expresso sections are about aerial precision — the glide mechanic creates float-focused challenges.

winky.description: Frog. Extremely high jump — highest of any buddy or character.
winky.jump_notes: Can jump significantly higher than any Kong or other buddy. Can stomp spiky enemies others cannot.
winky.notes: Winky sections are about vertical reach and navigating tall cavern structures.

enguarde.description: Swordfish. Used exclusively in underwater levels. Attacks with bill lunge.
enguarde.notes: Underwater physics apply. Enguarde provides combat capability in underwater sections where standard moves are ineffective.

squawks.description: Parrot. Carries a lantern in dark cave sections. Cannot be ridden as a mount in DKC1.
squawks.notes: Squawks illuminates Torchlight Trouble (cave level with near-total darkness). Strictly a visibility mechanic, not a movement change.

donkey_kong_standing.notes: DK is approximately 2×2 tiles in effective size. Hitbox narrower than sprite. Exact values need ROM verification.

diddy_kong_standing.notes: Diddy is approximately 1×1.5 tiles effective size. Narrower than DK.

_verification_checklist: Confirm roll speed from DKC Atlas ROM data (community estimate ~4-5 px/f)
Confirm Diddy vs DK jump height difference (if any)
design_notes_for_giants_drink: The dual-gravity system (0.28125 hold / 0.4375 release) is the defining physics feature of DKC. This exact ratio MUST be reproduced for the feel to be right — not just 'variable height jump.' The snappiness of releasing jump vs. the float of holding it is what players feel instinctively.

The high jump velocity (-7 px/frame) combined with variable gravity produces jumps that reach ~2.7 tiles (at full hold, 32px tiles) — significantly taller than Mario's typical 3-tile jump (16px tiles). Level design places platforms at DKC-appropriate heights.
DKC's 32×32 tile size reads very differently from 16×16. The world feels large and chunky. Character proportions relative to terrain are different — plan level geometry around the larger tile unit.

The two-character HP system means level design difficulty targets a 2-hit threshold, not 1-hit. The first hit (losing partner) serves as a warning; the second ends the run. Spiky-bottom pits are absolute because fall death bypasses the HP buffer.

Animal buddy sections function as mini-paradigm shifts within levels — each buddy has different movement capabilities, so level design becomes buddy-specific. This is a great source of within-level variety.
The roll/cartwheel as a speed tool (exceeding run speed) creates a risk/reward traversal choice: rolling is faster but harder to stop or redirect precisely.
